channel.title: Posts on EdwinBlommaerts.io
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on EdwinBlommaerts.io

item.title: Letters From Whitechapel : Boardgame
item.description: The date was Saturday 8 September 1988. I grabbed my Top Hat and medical bag as I rushed away from the body. I steadied myself as I walked onto Whitechapel High street and flagged down a carriage. Calmly I told the driver “Crossing between Aldgate High street and Hounds ditch, please.” while opening the carriage door and stepping inside. I quickly drew the curtains and sat back. As I felt the coach lurch away, I could hear a police whistle being blown.

item.title: Munchkin : Card Game
item.description: There we were, a dimly lit cave that smelled of stale water. My nostrils burned and my eyes were stinging as I inhaled the thick smoke left behind by the torch. Our group had ventured into the cave, winding our way deeper down into the earth. Our goal - the entrance to a forgotten dungeon rumoured to be located somewhere in this underground maze of tunnels and caverns. Our party consisted out of four adventurers.

item.title: Forbidden Island : Board Game
item.description: It was a rainy, cold day when I was heading to the helicopter pad in my car. David Bowie, Underground was playing on the radio. After weeks of preparation, planning and recruiting, the day had finally come. Just a week before today, I had finally managed to recruit a partner for this mad adventure. Damien, an engineer of un-matched skill and with an expertise in shoring up floods. Damien had travelled ahead of me to the island.

item.title: Boss Monster : Card Game
item.description: It was a dreary rainy day - we had just walked out of my local game shop and I felt disappointed. Most of the time they have a good assortment of recently released games, but none had tickled my fancy that day. As we turned the corner to enter the main street I suggested a short visit to the local Waterstones. Feeling glad to be out of the rain for a bit I browsed the store and eventually I ended up on the first floor.

item.title: Spiced black rum, Pink Floyd and Github.
item.description: It is pretty early in the morning when I write this. I found it hard to sleep after having a wonderful time at a relatives surprise birthday party. My body feels tired but my head still races on, as it often does. The evening was filled with pleasant conversation and rum. I somehow felt like drinking rum that evening. After scanning the cocktail menu, and finding the only one listed containing the sugar fermented alcohol was a rum flip cocktail, feeling adventurous I ordered one.

item.title: The first blog post
item.description: This is the first blog post of many to come. This will be my personal space on the vast internet. Most likely I will be writing about programming and board games. My interest in board games started when I was young and for SinterKlaas I asked my parents for a copy of Hero Quest. I remember coming home and seeing the box, I tore it open and pushed the cardboard prints out of the frames, twisted and turned the models to get them out of the plastic sprues and assembled the furniture and doors.
